skill.name: changelog-generator
skill.description: Automatically creates user-facing changelogs from git commits by analyzing commit history, categorizing changes, and transforming technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Turns hours of manual changelog writing into minutes of automated generation.
skill.summary: Turns Git commit history into clear release notes and changelog entries for users.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code fences around natural-language prompt examples. The weak-cryptography alerts are also false positives; the cited lines do not contain cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic operations. The remaining risk is low because the skill asks users to inspect Git history and may generate text for CHANGELOG.md, which can expose private commit details if published without review.

low_findings.title: Repository History May Contain Sensitive Details
low_findings.description: The skill directs the assistant to analyze Git history and produce changelog text. This is legitimate for release documentation, but private commit messages, security fixes, or internal issue names could be included in user-facing output if not reviewed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly says the skill scans Git history and suggests saving output to CHANGELOG.md. The risk is disclosure through generated text, not hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Command Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited regions are Markdown fenced examples and an example Markdown output block. No executable shell, Ruby, or script code appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are triple backtick Markdown fences, and the enclosed content is natural-language prompts or sample changelog text. There is no command invocation syntax or user-input execution path.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithms at the cited lines, but those lines contain the skill description, changelog behavior, sample release-note text, or related use cases. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no references to MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation on the cited lines. The alert appears to be a pattern-matching error.

content.value_statement: Release notes are hard to write when commits are technical and scattered. This skill turns Git history into clear changelogs for customers, teams, and release channels.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes commit history for a selected period or release range.
Groups changes into features, improvements, fixes, security updates, and breaking changes.
Rewrites technical commit messages into user-facing release notes.
Filters routine internal work such as tests and refactors from public updates.
Formats changelogs for release notes, app stores, emails, and update pages.
Can follow project-specific style guidance from a changelog style file.
limitations: Requires meaningful commit messages to produce accurate summaries.
Does not verify product behavior beyond the available Git history.
May omit important changes that were not represented in commits.
Generated text should be reviewed before publishing to customers.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Release Notes
use_cases.description: Create polished release notes from recent commits before a version launch.

use_cases.title: Maintain Public Changelogs
use_cases.description: Turn weekly or monthly development work into entries for CHANGELOG.md or a product updates page.

use_cases.title: Draft Channel-Specific Updates
use_cases.description: Adapt the same release content for app store submissions, email announcements, or internal summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Changelog
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a user-facing changelog from commits since the last release. Group the changes by feature, improvement, fix, and breaking change.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard release summary.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Date Range
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate release notes for commits between [start date] and [end date]. Use clear customer language and exclude routine internal work.

prompt_templates.title: Follow a Project Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a changelog for commits since [version]. Follow the tone and structure from CHANGELOG_STYLE.md, and flag any items that need human review.

prompt_templates.title: Build Multi-Channel Release Copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze commits from [range] and produce a full changelog, a short app store update, and an internal release summary. Separate public items from sensitive internal notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release workflow across several audiences.

output_examples.input: Commits from the past week include workspace sharing, faster sync, search indexing, and upload bug fixes.
output_examples.output: New Features: Team workspaces now support shared project spaces. Improvements: Sync is faster and search includes file contents. Fixes: Large image uploads are more reliable.

output_examples.input: A release range includes API cleanup, test refactors, a timezone fix, and a notification badge correction.
output_examples.output: Public changelog: Fixed timezone handling in scheduled posts and corrected notification badge counts. Internal notes: API cleanup and test refactors were excluded from customer-facing copy.

output_examples.input: Version 2.5.0 includes keyboard shortcuts, security hardening, and several small interface fixes.
output_examples.output: Version 2.5.0 adds keyboard shortcuts for faster navigation, improves security safeguards, and resolves small interface issues reported by users.

best_practices: Run the skill from the repository root so commit ranges are accurate.
Provide the release version, date range, or previous tag before generation.
Review output for sensitive commit details before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated changelogs without checking private issue names or security details.
Do not use vague commit histories as the only source for major release communication.
Do not mix internal engineering notes with customer-facing release notes.

faq.question: Does this skill write changelogs automatically?
faq.answer: It drafts changelog content from Git history. Users should review and approve the final text before publishing.

faq.question: Can it create release notes for a specific version?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the version, tag, date range, or previous release point.

faq.question: Does it support CHANGELOG.md?
faq.answer: Yes. It can produce content suitable for CHANGELOG.md and public update pages.

faq.question: Can it follow our writing style?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a style guide or changelog example for tone and structure.

faq.question: Will it include every commit?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to filter routine internal work and focus on user-relevant changes.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review generated text to remove private commit details, issue names, or unreleased security information.
